svg.text: Selection converts offered variation into movement — with no fitted constants
Qwen + OLMo selection loops; candidate value scores 0–1; 290 agreement-scored rounds
THE MODEL — one turn of the loop: variation in, movement out
refit output v_next becomes the next round’s generator
Sample candidate values for each prompt.
σ = mean across prompts of the
proxy for a, not an identity.
Train the next model on the kept set.
mixed pool: pool mean = (1−u)·host mean + u·supplier mean
sample → keep elites → update mean and variance (analogue, not the same algorithm)
Endpoint: iterate with a frozen selection boundary
Each round the kept mean lands one gap g = ρσ above the supply-updated
pool; the clip holds it inside the 0–1 score range. Re-measure ρ and σ
only when the judge, judging format, or pool policy changes.
THE EVIDENCE — the unit line fits without tuning; endpoints follow the same recurrence
forecast ρσ = judge/value agreement × offered spread
observed selector gap g (kept mean − pool mean)
— it uses the underlying normal SD, not the realized
One round ahead: forecast the next value
Predicting v_next before selection with the unit proxy is as
accurate as the fitted line — the extra constant buys nothing.
value MAE 0.0902 unit proxy vs 0.0891 fitted
Where the loop lands: unit recurrence + boundary refresh
fitted frozen-SD comparator 0.179 — swaps still the weak spot
37/38 large directions right (swap runs read from the swap boundary)
